violations.description: Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution between uses. -Bar sanitation bucket was under the acceptable range.

Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Nonfood contact surface of clean in place equipment did not meet proper design and/or construction characteristics. -Stand-up freezer in the back room has tape on the left door seals and the right door seals are falling apart. Suggest repairing seals.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Three compartment sink was not maintained or operated in the approved three step method. -Bar sanitation basin for the 3-sink system did not test to the minimum acceptable levels. Label of the Quaternary Ammonium being used in the establishment states 150-400 ppm.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. -Mop in the back room with the stand up freezer and ice machine were not hanging up and allowed for air drying.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food package was damaged; integrity of contents was compromised. *There was a box of spare ribs that were stored upright with a torn side of the box and the food product was in jeopardy of falling out onto the floor.--Found a tray of deserts that were not secured with several of them that were tipped and outside the rim of the lip of the tray. Make sure that prepared foods are secured so they do not spill out of their containers

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -The towel pail did not have a wiping cloth in it and a cloth for wiping down was not available. After discovery and when prompted they made one available.

Time as a public health control. — --Food was not served or discarded within four hours from the time when the food was removed from temperature control. -Discovered trays of cooked bacon that had been stored at room temperature for greater than 4 hours.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Discovered grease buildup on the shelves in most areas and accessories such as fans and the sides of counters.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -I observed a process by which the bacon was cooked, protected but stored at room temperature for greater than 4 hours. One packaged discovered was dated 9 days prior to the inspection. The bacon had been at room temperature since 3/23/25
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
